req: REQ-030 (Operations, Incident Response & Admin Correction Tooling)

issues.detail: The coverage row maps “founder-read daily is a documented human procedure” to AT-030.02, but that test only renders fixtures and probes dashboard uniqueness. It never verifies the requirement phrase “founder-read daily,” and the boundary note’s `[cross:]` has no delegation target.
issues.fix: Add a documentation assertion that the founder owns a daily money-dashboard review procedure with an explicit daily cadence.

issues.detail: AT-030.03 requires “NO operator-paging integration exists,” but PRD REQ-009 says that when provider usage is stale or a key flip cannot be confirmed, “the admin is paged.” Both cannot pass simultaneously.
issues.fix: Resolve the PRD conflict: either replace REQ-009 paging with a permitted notification mechanism or document an explicit watchdog exception in REQ-030 and narrow AT-030.03 accordingly.

issues.detail: The requirement says “every correction is balanced and traceable to its authoritative source” and “corrections show on the money dashboard.” AT-030.06 proves traceability and dashboard visibility only for one missed Stripe top-up; AT-030.07 does not assert those properties for chargeback, Anthropic-spend, or internal-pairing corrections.
issues.fix: For every divergence fixture in AT-030.07, assert a persisted reference to the applicable authoritative source and a corresponding money-dashboard correction entry; retain the REQ-006 delegation for arithmetic depth.

issues.detail: The requirement globally states “no correction UI,” whereas AT-030.06 asserts only “NO correction UI in the path.” An unused or separately reachable correction UI could exist while this test passes.
issues.fix: Add an application-wide absence probe covering routes, navigation, admin surfaces, and correction-action endpoints.

issues.detail: AT-030.08 labels the sole posting function “guarded, idempotent, audited,” but its observable checks establish only direct-write rejection and replay idempotency. No assertion demonstrates that a correction actually creates an audit record, so “audited” is not independently judgeable.
issues.fix: After posting a correction, assert one retrievable audit record tied to the correction and authoritative-source reference; also assert that replay does not create a second posting audit event.
